name: Bacardi Ltd.
lede: Bacardi runs four capability centers across four Indian cities. Ghaziabad, Gurugram, Nashik and Mysuru, covering gbs, manufacturing and R&D, built over 30 years.
insight: Search elsewhere and you will usually find one Bacardi office in India. We map four, across four cities, each tagged by center type and function. Knowing the whole footprint is what turns a name on a list into an account you can plan against.
metaTitle: Bacardi Ltd. GCC in India: Centers, Footprint & Leaders | Bamboo Reports
metaDescription: Bacardi runs 4 verified GCC centers across 4 Indian cities (Ghaziabad, Gurugram, Nashik, Mysuru), covering gbs, manufacturing and R&D. See the full India footprint, org map and contacts on Bamboo Reports.

leaders.departments: Finance, HR, GCC leadership and Procurement

about.description: Bacardi's India footprint covers Ghaziabad, Gurugram, Nashik and Mysuru, spanning gbs, manufacturing and R&D. The centers deliver IT, R&D and ER&D, Finance & Accounting and HR for the group's global operations.

bandCopy: Exact center locations, per-center headcount, the technology stack and all eleven decision-makers, in one profile.

faq.q: How many GCC centers does Bacardi operate in India?
faq.a: Bacardi operates 4 active Global Capability Center sites in India.

faq.q: Which Indian cities does Bacardi have GCC centers in?
faq.a: Bacardi's India GCC footprint spans Ghaziabad, Gurugram, Nashik and Mysuru.

faq.q: What types of GCC centers does Bacardi run in India?
faq.a: Bacardi runs gbs, manufacturing and R&D centers in India.

faq.q: How many people work at Bacardi's India GCCs?
faq.a: Bacardi's India centers employ in the 501 to 1,000 range. The exact aggregate and the headcount at each site are in the full profile.

faq.q: Who leads Bacardi's GCC in India?
faq.a: Bamboo Reports tracks 11 leaders at Bacardi's India centers, across Finance, HR, GCC leadership and Procurement. Names and contact details open with a free account.

sources: company filings, LinkedIn, NASSCOM and Bamboo Reports' primary research
